overview: Mono-Green Midrange in Modern leverages rapid mana acceleration to deploy large, resilient threats ahead of the opponent's curve. The deck uses Noble Hierarch to generate early mana and tokens, while Badgermole Cub provides turn-one board presence and consistent card draw. Green Sun's Zenith serves as the primary engine for value, allowing you to fetch multiple threats and outlast opponents in extended games.
win_conditions: Outpacing opponents by casting large creatures on turn three using Noble Hierarch and other mana dorks
Generating infinite card advantage and board presence through repeated Green Sun's Zenith casts
Evolving Badgermole Cub into a massive threat that draws cards and closes out games
Flood the zone with tokens and wide boards to overwhelm control or combo strategies

keep_criteria: Hands containing two or more lands and a turn-one mana dork or Badgermole Cub
Hands with Noble Hierarch and a ramp spell to ensure turn-three threats
Hands featuring Green Sun's Zenith with enough mana sources to cast it by turn three
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only lands and no creatures or acceleration
Hands lacking early board presence or card draw
Hands with multiple high-cost threats but insufficient mana to deploy them

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and mana dorks to accelerate into turn-three threats
Deploy Badgermole Cub immediately to establish board presence and draw into answers
Use Noble Hierarch to generate mana and create tokens for early blocks or trades
mid_game: Cast Green Sun's Zenith to fetch key threats that match or exceed the opponent's curve
Trade efficiently with opponent's creatures while maintaining a mana advantage
Use ramp spells to stay ahead on the curve and pressure the opponent's resources
late_game: Flood the board with large creatures and tokens to apply overwhelming combat damage
Recast Green Sun's Zenith repeatedly to refill your hand and maintain pressure
Close out games by leveraging unblockable attackers or massive board states

key_cards.name: Badgermole Cub
key_cards.role: Early board presence and card advantage
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one or two to establish a threat. Use its draw ability to find ramp or removal, and let it grow into a larger threat over time.

key_cards.name: Green Sun's Zenith
key_cards.notes: Use to fetch key threats when you have excess mana. Can also be used to replace itself or fetch removal in a pinch, ensuring you never run out of resources.

key_cards.name: Noble Hierarch
key_cards.notes: Deploy early to ramp into turn-three spells. Use its token ability to block, trade, or generate additional mana for big plays.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing Noble Hierarch before casting Green Sun's Zenith to maximize available mana for fetching threats.
Hold back interaction until you see what the opponent is playing, as mono-green midrange relies on reading the board state.
Use Badgermole Cub's card draw to find ramp spells, ensuring you stay ahead on the curve.
Sequence Green Sun's Zenith after playing your lands to avoid mana screw and ensure you can cast the fetched creature immediately.

matchups.advice: Focus on early blockers and use Noble Hierarch tokens to stall. Prioritize fetching wide threats to outvalue them.
Play around counterspells by holding up mana. Use Green Sun's Zenith to fetch threats that can pressure their hand or board.
Disrupt their setup with early interaction. Use Badgermole Cub to draw into answers and apply pressure before they assemble their combo.
Race efficiently by leveraging mana advantage. Use Green Sun's Zenith to fetch threats that match or exceed their curve.
